Bio: Legend has it that Jess haunts the moors of Salem. She gives out stickers with her cats in them to the deserving. This is her corporeal form's 11th year performing with PMRP.

Stephen returns for his second show with PMRP for Dangerous Adventures. Previously he was part of the ensemble for last summer's 13th Mystery: The Black Pearl. Previously he has appeared on the Travel Channel's paranormal themed reality show The Dead Files, Malden Community Theater's Summer 2023 production of 35MM: A Musical Exhibition and the Theatre Company of Saugus's production of Puffs where he played more than a dozen different roles. He'd also like to thank his wife and son for their support. His two cats couldn't care less.

Sue Downing (she/they, understudy Omar) has previously been a voice actor in three different Halloween shows with PMRP. Sue also directed “Family Business” by Tegan Garon on behalf of PMRP sponsorship in the 2023 Boston Theater Marathon XXV.
Erika Claire is so excited to be a part of Townies and Dragons! She would like to thank Paul and Erikson for letting her hang out with them.

Jaimie Carlson adapted and assistant directed The Most Dangerous Game. When not brutally dispatching a cantaloupe as a Foley artist for PMRP, Jaimie is probably wrangling robots with ruthless efficiency in her job as an engineer, or testing her skills in the least dangerous game (quizbowl). Jaimie can be tracked down to her habitat of downtown Boston, where she stalks her natural prey of overpriced coffee and underpriced books. She thanks all the PMRPers for showing her the ropes behind-the-scenes, and her family for coming out to the show!

Francine Davis (Director, "The Bonnie Tales of Captain Bellamy") is a Boston-based actor/director who has been involved in over 150 productions, working with such companies as King Richard's Faire; Olde Essex Stage Company; Yorick Ensemble; and Boston Shakespeare Project Collective. Her directing credits include: "Romeo and Juliet;" "Much Ado About Nothing;" and "The Women of Lockerbie." Favorite acting roles include: Kate in "The Taming of the Shrew;" "Cassius in "Julius Caesar;" and Hannah in "The Spitfire Grill." Francine is happy to be working with PMRP for the third time. Last spring, she made her Foley debut (what fun!) with PRMP, and for the group's Halloween show, she directed "The Inexperienced Ghost."

Jack Wickwire (Ivan—Dangerous Game, Foley—Townies and Dragons) has acted and designed for many MetroWest community theaters. His acting credits include: Mortimer in "Arsenic and Old Lace," (Groton Theater); Paul in "My Three Angels" (Vokes Theater); Inspector Hound in "The Real Inspector Hound," (Belmont Theater); Hamlet in "Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead" (Concord Players); Prince John in "Lion in Winter" (Groton Theater); Makeup Design for "The Inexperienced Ghost" (PMRP), Picasso at the Lapin Agile, Lend Me a Tenor (TCAN), Prelude to a Kiss, Waiting in the Wings, (for Belmont Belmont Dramatic Club); "Is He Dead", "Sunday in the Park With George" (Vokes Theater); Batboy, The Musical" (Arlington Friends of the Drama).

Jack Wickwire (Ivan—Dangerous Game, Foley—Townies and Dragons) has acted and designed for many MetroWest community theaters. His acting credits include: Mortimer in "Arsenic and Old Lace," (Groton Theater); Paul in "My Three Angels" (Vokes Theater); Inspector Hound in "The Real Inspector Hound," (Belmont Theater); Hamlet in "Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead" (Concord Players); Prince John in "Lion in Winter" (Groton Theater); Makeup Design for "The Inexperienced Ghost" (PMRP), Picasso at the Lapin Agile, Lend Me a Tenor (TCAN), Prelude to a Kiss, Waiting in the Wings, (for Belmont Belmont Dramatic Club); "Is He Dead", "Sunday in the Park With George" (Vokes Theater); Batboy, The Musical" (Arlington Friends of the Drama).

Kristina Spinney is an actor turned accountant in the live music industry who is thrilled to be making her PMRP debut. She holds a BFA in acting from Salem State University, has produced film festivals and most recently, appeared on sci-fi, horror, and film panels at Arisia this past winter. Some past credits include Maria in West Side Story, Lilli Vanessi in Kiss Me, Kate, Antigone in Antigone and Penelope Pennywise in Urinetown. Kristina enjoys horror, sci-fi, and fantasy of all types. You can typically find Kristina at a book club, cult cinema night, or dancing at a goth club. LLAP!

Hannah (she/her) is thrilled to be making her PMRP debut in Dangerous Adventures! Some highlight previous roles include a bottle dancer/ensemble (Fiddler on the Roof), Pig #1 (Shrek the Musical), Snug (A Midsummer Night’s Dream), and Valentine (Mary Poppins), all with Needham Community Theatre, as well as Barbara (boom) at Worcester Polytechnic Institute. When she’s not onstage, Hannah works as a controls/test engineer and can usually be found rowing, hiking, flinging herself around the ninja gym, and laboriously re-learning how to play the violin. Yarrr!!

Ray is excited to be making his acting debut with PMRP! He's found a new love for the theater, and can't wait to show the city of Boston. When not on stage, you can find him at the nearest thrift shop looking for the busiest looking shirts he can get his hands on. He also likes to browse local record stores and watch silly movies with his friends.

Alex is excited to be returning to the mic for his 8th production with PMRP. Previous roles with PMRP have included Nick Charles (The Thin Man), Desmond Cleese (Women Behind the Curtain) and Clay (The Inexperienced Ghost). 

Alex would like to thank his partner Jazz, his family, and his much beloved cats Kevin and Mylo for their unending love and support.

Name Pronunciation: Day-Vid Mer-Go (accent first syllable in both)
Bio: David has been cast as Ronny in "Townies and Dragons: A Barrel Full of Peril". He previously played the roles of Dr. Kemp and Mariner in “Invisible Man” in PMRP’s “Unseen Worlds of H.G.Wells. He has performed as Kris Kringle in Colonial Radio Players (ccp1961.org) radio/stage production of "Miracle on 34th Street," joined them again for their 2023 production of "It's a Wonderful Life" in a variety of roles, including Nick the Bartender, Joseph the Angel, Henry Potter, and others, and played seven+ characters in their 2024 production of “Miracle on 34th Street.” He looks forward to future performances with PMRP. This one's for Em, Rachel, and Chris!

Theresa is pleased to be making her debut with PMRP! Favorite past performances include Lyubov in “The Cherry Orchard,” and Big Stone in Eurydice, both with Theatre@First. Off-stage, Theresa is a medical writer and childless cat lady.

Michael McAfee has been active with the PMRP since the very first show, and he has served in various capacities ever since.  He also helps backstage with the "Mrs. Hawking" series of plays, and is active on the local poetry open mic circuit.  By day, he is a software quality engineer, but is always on the lookout for awesome projects to contribute to.
This is Nick's first PMRP show, and first time on stage since high school over 20 years ago. It's much better than other things he could be doing during his mid-life crisis.

Brian is a writer, actor and physical comedian previously seen as Uncle Jacob the Dancing Zombie in Hopkinton's "Deadwoodville."  His audio plays are sometimes heard on the podcast "Stories Found" and he recommends the show highly for this and other reasons (including their love of cryptids.)

This is Andrew's second show with PMRP! Andrew loves all things music, theater, and the arts and works as a mechanical engineer by day, He is also the bassist and vocalist for local band Good Day for Greg. He's positively thrilled to conquer the high seas as Wolfie in this production!

Maeve is thrilled to be returning to PMRP for their second show! You can find her acting out around Greater Boston - previous roles include Fruma Sarah in Fiddler on the Roof (Needham Community Theatre), the Baker in Into the Woods (Colonial Chorus Players), and Julia Wolfe/Inspector Guild in the 13th Mystery with PMRP. They are a student of animation at MassArt.

Joev is brogueing and boying in his thirtieth show with the PMRP. You can join Joev for improvisational free fitness hilarity with November Project Boston ( https://november-project.com/boston/ ) any time, as long as that time is MWF 6:30am. Just show up!

Naomi Ibasitas (Sully, Barrel Full of Peril) is an actor, stage manager, and director within the Boston fringe theatre community.  She is excited to be back on the PMRP stage!  Previously you may have seen her as Nora Charles in The Thin Man, Lieutenant Sulu in Gender-Swapped Star Trek, Lupe in La Siguanaba, or as Tiger Lily in Peter Pan! 

Non-PMRP acting credits include Disenchanted! (Mulan/Pocahontas) with the Walpole Footlighters, the Annual Asian American Playwrights Collective Festival 2024 (various roles), and 7 Rooms: The Masque of the Red Death (Misha in Toasting Man) with Flat Earth Theatre. She is also very involved in the Mrs. Hawking play series playing various roles (please go check it out on YouTube!). Off the stage, she is a character performer with Spark a Dream Princess Parties!
This is Arturo's first PMRP show and first time doing foley work. He has experience acting in several shows in college more than a decade ago, and he's happy to be performing again!

Sid has been peripherally involved with PMRP for a few years but this is their first time being involved in a production from start to finish after being tricked into it by the Foley coordinator, who happens to be their roommate. When not herding actors, Sid spends their time herding their two elderly cats and herding themself toward finishing their bachelor's degree a mere eleven and a half years behind schedule.

Racer Soares, 24, resides in Arlington and has been involved in local theatre and music for over fifteen years. This is his 3rd show with PMRP doing foley arts. Racer has recently graduated from UMass Lowell for  Composition for New Media. When he isn’t playing in a band, tutoring, or doing camera work. He enjoys grilling on the bar-be-que and walking his dog, Ringo. Racer would like to thank his family for their support.
Meg is a weird kid.
Jeremy has been working with the PMRP for over a decade now, and is their current Artistic Director.  He's currently preparing for his 12th year of the PMRP Summer Mysteries (come out and audition!)

Despite his current prison sentence for five too many Presidential impersonations, Jacob Sommer is pleased to be helping serve the PMRP community as assistant for the House. When not serving time for silly voices, Jacob is a husband and father who serves the wider state community, studies part-time for his Master’s degree in public administration, ponders politics and policy, plays games, and watches perhaps too much anime. He looks forward to earning his degree and having more time to assist or perform in shows.
This is Terry’s first production with PMRP, and a return to theater after about twenty years. He has wanted to learn Foley for quite some time and is grateful to PMRP for the opportunity!

Tori is thrilled to be working on their 20th production with PMRP, with Dangerous Adventures marking their first forray into producing. They would like to thank the entire foley team for their dedication and hard work, Jaimie Carlson for writing such a foley-loving script, their partner (Jon Mendoza) for putting up with their theater-induced stress, their best friend (Sid Hackney) for allowing themself to be dragged into PMRP, and their beautiful and evil cat, Persephone, for always being there to bite knees.

Jonathan Mendoza is a Massachusetts-based actor/director, returning for his second time directing and his fifth overall outing with the Post-Meridian Radio Players. He made his directorial debut in 2024's "The Unseen Worlds of H. G. Wells," as director for "The Invisible Man" and assistant director for "The Stolen Body." Jonathan is excited to return to directing for PMRP, and is pleasantly surprised to have been invited back after having contributing practically nothing but aggressively yowling in what passed for direction during a previous WGBH spotlight, which he thought everyone else was quite eloquent and lovely in.

Jay Sekora (sound engineer) has worked with PMRP as an actor and director as well as working on sound. He directed PMRP’s adaptations of “THEM!”, “Metropolis”, and “Filibus”, and adapted and directed “Night of the Living Dead”. His favorite PMRP acting roles were a drunken astronaut in “Junkyard” and the homicidal narrator in “The Tell-Tale Heart”. And he’s done a lot of sound engineering and other sound-tech work.  He thanks his beloved Mare Freed for getting him back into theater after a long absence, and for a wonderful shared life together.

Name Pronunciation: Row-zee Sent-Man (pronounced exactly like the words 'sent' and 'man')
Bio: Rosie Sentman is so excited to be working with PRMP for the first time! They are an actor, voice actor, singer, and all around 'theatre artist', originally from Georgia and now based in Boston. Recently, they performed as M'ling in Theatre@First's adaptation of The Island of Dr. Moreau, 'None Escape'; up next, they will be narrating a second short story for the Escape Artists Foundation's sci-fi podcast magazine, Escape Pod. Find more info or contact them at rosiesentman.com.

Emma is delighted to be making her Boston community theatre debut after a long performance hiatus. Originally from Cleveland, OH she is grateful for everyone here in Boston welcoming her with open arms and forgiving the many midwestern goodbyes they are subjected to.

Name Pronunciation: I've never had to explain this one lol
Bio: This is now Jason's third show with PMRP having started with the HG Wells Unseen Worlds. When not on stage he does funny voices in the woods, and talks about Food at great length whether you want him to or not.
